transcript.text: Hello, living in Lafayette, V-log, 22nd of June 2013.
It's 10 o'clock at night. I just got back. I've been in Boulder all day.
Got up this morning and rode into town to mow my friend's lawn and then water's plants.
Then went in and did a bunch of stuff for my mom, fixed a cabinet door, moved a painting around,
Organized her closet, took a bunch of trash out, vacuumed the house, that kind of stuff.
And then as a reward, she took me to see Superman, Man of Steel.
A really good movie, man. Oh, yeah, really long, but really good, man.
Total action. Superman's a badass.
And then she drove me home, yay, with some other stuff that I had there at my mom's house.
Because last night I saw Goal 3, because I really liked Goal 1 and Goal 2 was pretty good,
But Goal 3 was the most confusing movie I've ever seen, dude.
But the dude Santiago Munoz, the soccer player, and it's like it had nothing to do with the first movie.
The only similarity was that it was the same actor playing the same character,
But he was just the side character. It wasn't even about him.
They didn't even play any soccer in the whole movie.
It was about his friends, who were his friends from the soccer team.
And one of them's about to get married, but then he dies.
And then after he dies, they're in one of the qualifiers for the World Cup, but then they lose.
And then the other one gets married, and that's the movie.
And I was like, what the fuck?
It was like they had a contract to make three movies, and they didn't feel like doing it,
So they decided to just do some other side story or something.
Anyway, I like my movies.
There's a lot of economic movies, documentaries on YouTube.
I want to start watching those.
But, oh yeah, I'm tired, though.
Tomorrow, I don't have to do anything, so tomorrow I'm going to work on my timeline all day long,
But it's going to be cool to not have to exercise.
You know, we get to recover the body some.
So I got my V-log in for today.
That's about all I have to say.
Peace out.
Oh, and I want to start getting in the habit of doing lots of request videos.
People are sending me cool request videos,
And I'm starting to get in the habit of, I think, making more videos.
A little bit more balanced life, because, you know, I'm working now and stuff,
So I can balance stuff out a little bit more.
But I still have the fever.
I still have the fever for the timeline.
Okay, peace out.
